demographics.living_situation: lives at home with his wife, who is his primary carer

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Call made by his exhausted wife) He hasn't slept in two days, he doesn't know who I am anymore, and I physically cannot stop him from trying to unlock the front door. He keeps saying 'I have to go to work right now, my boss is waiting for me' and pacing up and down. I don't know what to do — I can't keep him safe on my own tonight.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He has been awake and pacing for about two days and keeps trying to leave the house at night. Tonight he doesn't recognise me and is getting more agitated at the door, and I'm at breaking point.

symptom_narrative.onset: The confusion has been getting worse over about three years, but this bad spell of no sleep and constant pacing started roughly two days ago and is worse tonight
symptom_narrative.duration: Awake and pacing for around 48 hours; tonight is the worst it has been
symptom_narrative.character: He is restless, pacing the hallway, repeatedly going to the front door and trying to unlock it, and pulling away the chairs I put in front of it. He believes he has to go to work and thinks it is years ago
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: His wife says she has never seen him this agitated or this hard to settle, and she cannot physically manage him alone
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: Nothing he is pointing to as painful — it is his behaviour and confusion
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: Much worse in the evening and overnight; he will not sleep
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: Nothing so far tonight; blocking the door just makes him more agitated
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: The evening and night, and being told he is wrong about needing to go to work
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: Tonight he did not recognise his wife of 40 years, which frightened them both; he has had increasing word-finding trouble and can no longer work the TV remote over the past year
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: He wanders and gets confused in the evenings sometimes, but has never gone this long without sleep or been this hard to redirect
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: Needs help with all day-to-day tasks like managing money and planning the day; physically he is still strong and mobile, which is part of why she cannot hold him back

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Long-standing dementia diagnosed about three years ago, with steadily worsening memory
past_medical_history.how_managed: under the GP, on a memory tablet

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Retired for about 20 years; married 40 years; his wife is his sole carer and is physically and emotionally exhausted after two nights without sleep

communication_style.typical_message_style: The wife is the informant; she is frightened, exhausted and asking for help to get through the night safely

objective_data_offered.value: He has been awake and pacing for about two days and will not sleep; tonight he does not recognise me and keeps trying to unlock the front door and pull away the chairs I've put in front of it
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: Carer's account under acute stress; no objective measurement

objective_data_offered.value: I don't think he has a fever, he's not coughing, he's not complaining of any pain and hasn't had a fall that I saw; he's been eating and drinking a bit today
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: Carer impression only — no thermometer reading taken and a frail patient can have an infection without obvious signs; does not exclude an underlying medical cause

objective_data_offered.value: On the video he is up and walking about, looks physically well and steady on his feet, but is restless and keeps going back toward the door
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: Brief video glimpse only, not a physical examination
